results.possibleIdentity: Michael Snyder was an electronics engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, based in Somerville, New Jersey. He was employed by Mepco Incorporated (also known as Mepco, Inc.), a manufacturer of precision electronic devices such as resistors, capacitors, and microcircuits with facilities in Morristown and Somerville, New Jersey. Snyder was a technical member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 by engineers Billy Klüver and Fred Waldhauer and artists Robert Rauschenberg and Robert Whitman to foster collaborations between the arts and the scientific and engineering communities. As an electronics engineer, he was part of E.A.T.'s technical services program, which maintained a directory of engineers to assist artists with technical expertise in their creative projects.
